# Cool TravelPal growth operations

This is the working checklist for publishing, distribution, measurement, and monetization.

## Brand accounts

Use the same identity wherever possible.

- Display name: `Cool TravelPal`
- X handle: `@cooltravelpal`
- YouTube handle: `@CoolTravelPal`
- YouTube channel: `https://www.youtube.com/@CoolTravelPal`
- Website: `https://cooltravelpal.com/`
- Newsletter: `https://cooltravelpal.com/newsletter/`
- Avatar: `images/travelpal-logo.png`

### X profile

Bio:

> Travel smarter. Earn better. Experience more. Practical travel guides, points advice, books, museums, and privacy-first travel apps.

Pinned launch post:

> Welcome to Cool TravelPal. We publish thoughtful city stories, practical travel strategies, points guidance, book and museum notes, and privacy-first travel tools. Start exploring: https://cooltravelpal.com/

Initial cadence: three useful posts per week. Most posts should contain a complete observation or recommendation, not only an article link.

### YouTube channel

Description:

> Cool TravelPal helps you travel smarter, earn better, and experience more. Expect practical destination guides, points-and-miles explanations, books connected to places, museums worth slowing down for, and honest looks at privacy-first travel apps from TravelPal LLC.
>
> Read the guides at https://cooltravelpal.com/ and join the occasional newsletter at https://cooltravelpal.com/newsletter/.

Pilot formats:

1. A concise city, museum, or road-trip guide based on a published article.
2. A book review connected to the city in which it was read or discussed.
3. A practical travel, points, or travel-technology explanation.

Publish three pilot videos before committing to a fixed schedule.

## Article intake

For each source post, record:

- Source URL and original title
- City or destination
- Category: travel, books, museums, culture, points, or apps
- Original Chinese text
- Images owned by TravelPal LLC
- Any facts that need current-source verification
- Proposed English reader angle

Include lesser-known Chinese places and books when the English article clearly explains why the subject matters to a reader unfamiliar with it.

## Article publication checklist

- Original English title, introduction, and structure
- City and category labels
- Unique meta description and canonical URL
- Open Graph image and descriptive alt text
- Article and breadcrumb structured data
- Link to the relevant city hub
- Two or three useful internal links
- Sitemap entry and RSS item
- Newsletter link available in the global footer
- Mobile layout checked
- No unnecessary Xiaohongshu attribution for content owned by TravelPal LLC
- No image dominated by untranslated Chinese text unless the text is editorially relevant and explained

## Images and media

- Prefer original owned photographs when they strengthen the article.
- Otherwise use licensed copyright-free city images or original generated illustrations.
- Convert page images to WebP or AVIF and aim for roughly 300 KB or less when quality permits.
- Keep large originals only when needed for social previews or archival purposes.
- Continue using GitHub Pages while repository size and bandwidth remain modest; review external media hosting only when growth makes it necessary.

## Newsletter

- Welcome automation: active
- Double opt-in: tested
- Recommended campaign cadence: monthly or twice monthly
- Each digest: one lead city story, one book/culture item, one practical travel or points item, and a brief personal introduction
- Test every campaign on desktop and mobile before sending

## Measurement

Weekly during launch:

- GA4 traffic, acquisition source, and top landing pages
- Google Search Console indexing, queries, and click-through rate
- EmailOctopus subscriber growth, delivery, clicks, bounces, and unsubscribes

Monthly after launch:

- Refresh weak titles and descriptions when impressions grow without clicks
- Add internal links from strong pages to newer related articles
- Expand city hubs that have gained additional articles

## Search distribution

- Keep Google Search Console sitemap active
- Add the same sitemap to Bing Webmaster Tools
- Maintain clear authorship, source-supported current facts, structured data, city hubs, and descriptive headings for both conventional and AI-assisted search
- Do not add speculative AI-search files or markup without a clear supported use

## Referrals and affiliates

Begin only after the site has a useful library and measurable reader intent.

Before adding compensated links:

- Create a clear affiliate disclosure
- Select only programs directly relevant to an article
- Keep editorial conclusions independent of compensation
- Track outbound clicks without collecting email addresses or sensitive data in analytics
- Review each program's current rules before publishing links

## Quarterly maintenance

- Check internal and external links
- Review Core Web Vitals and image weight
- Test keyboard navigation and mobile layouts
- Review sitemap, RSS, structured data, and index coverage
- Refresh time-sensitive travel and points information
- Review privacy, copyright, and affiliate disclosures
- Back up original media and newsletter assets
